writing_points.claim: Welcome to Dice Pyramid, a solo game of exploration and skill!
writing_points.source_text: Welcome to Dice Pyramid, a solo game of exploration and skill!

writing_points.claim: Journey deep into the heart of an ancient Egyptian pyramid, where each room does present a new challenge that will test your luck and strategy.
writing_points.source_text: Journey deep into the heart of an ancient Egyptian pyramid, where each room does present a new challenge that will test your luck and strategy.

writing_points.claim: Roll the dice and match the challenges you face, from Unstable Stairways to Hall of Hieroglyphics, get powerful relics as you complete rooms and climb your way to the top of the pyramid and the ultimate prize: the Treasure Chamber containing the final riddle.
writing_points.source_text: Roll the dice and match the challenges you face, from Unstable Stairways to Hall of Hieroglyphics, get powerful relics as you complete rooms and climb your way to the top of the pyramid and the ultimate prize: the Treasure Chamber containing the final riddle.

writing_points.claim: Are you ready to accept the challenge and conquer the pyramid?
writing_points.source_text: Are you ready to accept the challenge and conquer the pyramid?

writing_points.claim: Each card is split into two parts: a room and a relic.
writing_points.source_text: Each card is split into two parts: a room and a relic. Only one part -the one on top- is active at a time.

writing_points.claim: The cards are placed in the pyramid as rooms.
writing_points.source_text: The cards are placed in the pyramid as rooms. When you complete a room, you place it on your side and rotate the card, so the relic side is on top.

writing_points.claim: Room side A room has 3 sections: name, challenge and description.
writing_points.source_text: Room side A room has 3 sections: name, challenge and description.

writing_points.claim: The challenge is what you need to do to complete the room.
writing_points.source_text: The challenge is what you need to do to complete the room. It is displayed using images (see “Reading the cards” for details). The description is a readable version of the challenge

writing_points.claim: Relic side A relic also has 3 sections: name, ability and description.
writing_points.source_text: Relic side A relic also has 3 sections: name, ability and description.

writing_points.claim: The ability is the effect that this relic provides.
writing_points.source_text: The ability is the effect that this relic provides. It is displayed using images (see “Reading the cards” for details). The description is a readable version of the ability

writing_points.claim: Take out the Treasure Chamber and Entrance rooms.
writing_points.source_text: Take out the Treasure Chamber and Entrance rooms. Shuffle the remaining cards.

writing_points.claim: Build 2 rows under it with 2 and 3 cards respectively in the shape of a pyramid like this:
writing_points.source_text: Place the Treasure Chamber on the table, face down. Build 2 rows under it with 2 and 3 cards respectively in the shape of a pyramid like this:

writing_points.claim: To build this row, take 3 cards and shuffle them with the Entrance.
writing_points.source_text: The rooms on the last row are the Initial Rooms that are available at the beginning of the game. To build this row, take 3 cards and shuffle them with the Entrance. Place them face up.

writing_points.claim: For optional rules and alternative setups, check the section “Optional Rules”.
writing_points.source_text: For optional rules and alternative setups, check the section “Optional Rules”.

writing_points.claim: You play turns until you obtain the Golden Ankh (complete the Treasure Chamber) or fail to complete a room with no relics left.
writing_points.source_text: You play turns until you obtain the Golden Ankh (complete the Treasure Chamber) or fail to complete a room with no relics left.

writing_points.claim: Twice per turn, you can re-roll any number of dice.
writing_points.source_text: Twice per turn, you can re-roll any number of dice.

writing_points.claim: You can use the ability of any relic you own anytime.
writing_points.source_text: You can use the ability of any relic you own anytime. A relic is discarded when used.

writing_points.claim: You can’t have more than 5 relics.
writing_points.source_text: If your dice match the challenge of a room, you can take the pyramid card to your side and rotate it so the relic part is up. You can’t have more than 5 relics. If you obtain more, choose and discard down to 5.

writing_points.claim: If your dice match several challenges, you chose which one to complete.
writing_points.source_text: If your dice match several challenges, you chose which one to complete.

writing_points.claim: When you take a pyramid card, if it was the last card covering a room, that room is now open.
writing_points.source_text: When you take a pyramid card, if it was the last card covering a room, that room is now open. Flip it face up. Next turn you can also attempt its challenge.

writing_points.claim: If at the end of the turn your dice do not match any challenge, discard a relic to start a new turn.
writing_points.source_text: If at the end of the turn your dice do not match any challenge, discard a relic to start a new turn. If you don’t have any relics to discard, you lose.

writing_points.claim: When (if) you match the challenge of the Treasure Chamber, obtaining the Golden Ankh relic, you win.
writing_points.source_text: When (if) you match the challenge of the Treasure Chamber, obtaining the Golden Ankh relic, you win.

writing_points.claim: While both the rooms and the relic have a description that explains what the cards does, most of the card is used by the hieroglyphics that represent both the challenge and the relic ability.
writing_points.source_text: While both the rooms and the relic have a description that explains what the cards does, most of the card is used by the hieroglyphics that represent both the challenge and the relic ability.

writing_points.claim: They are easy to decipher, and the game is more fun when you use them.
writing_points.source_text: They are easy to decipher, and the game is more fun when you use them. Here is how to read them:

writing_points.claim: means one dice with any value.
writing_points.source_text: means one dice with any value. If it appears multiple times, each of them can be any value.

writing_points.claim: mean a dice with a specific value.
writing_points.source_text: mean a dice with a specific value. If it appears multiple times, all of them must have the same value.

writing_points.claim: : Re-roll: pick a dice and roll it again
writing_points.source_text: : Re-roll: pick a dice and roll it again

writing_points.claim: : Flip: turn a dice upside down, a 1 becomes a 6, a 2 becomes a 5, etc.
writing_points.source_text: : Flip: turn a dice upside down, a 1 becomes a 6, a 2 becomes a 5, etc.

writing_points.claim: Power Relics, Small Backpack You can use the relics every turn without discarding, but can only carry 2 of them
writing_points.source_text: Power Relics, Small Backpack You can use the relics every turn without discarding, but can only carry 2 of them

writing_points.claim: Exploring the Great Pyramid This game plays a pyramid with 5 rows where the Treasure Chamber can be anywhere (except on the initial row).
writing_points.source_text: Exploring the Great Pyramid This game plays a pyramid with 5 rows where the Treasure Chamber can be anywhere (except on the initial row).

writing_points.claim: Separate the Treasure Chamber and the Entrance, shuffle the remaining cards and separate 4 of them for the initial row, add the Entrance to them.
writing_points.source_text: Separate the Treasure Chamber and the Entrance, shuffle the remaining cards and separate 4 of them for the initial row, add the Entrance to them.

writing_points.claim: Use the deck to build the 4 levels of the pyramid, then make the initial row with the cards you separated initially.
writing_points.source_text: Shuffle the Treasure Chamber into the deck. Use the deck to build the 4 levels of the pyramid, then make the initial row with the cards you separated initially.

writing_points.claim: I know the way Play with the pyramid cards face up.
writing_points.source_text: I know the way Play with the pyramid cards face up. You can plan ahead knowing the challenges ahead of you.

selected_writing_points.claim: Welcome to Dice Pyramid, a solo game of exploration and skill!
selected_writing_points.source_text: Welcome to Dice Pyramid, a solo game of exploration and skill!
